question_text: A roller coaster car at the top of a hill has 15,000 J of gravitational potential energy. At the bottom of the hill, it has 12,000 J of kinetic energy. Calculate the energy that has been dissipated due to friction and air resistance.
explanation: The energy dissipated equals the initial gravitational potential energy minus the final kinetic energy: 15,000 J − 12,000 J = 3,000 J. This energy is lost as heat and sound due to friction and air resistance.
